---
title: "🌉 Introducing kakehashi VSCode extension — Bringing intelligent features to Markdown code blocks such as completions, hovers, diagnostics, and more"
author: atusy
date: '2026-05-19'
slug: kakehashi-vscode
categories: [Tech]
tags: [VSCode, Kakehashi]
output:
  blogdown::html_page:
    md_extensions: +east_asian_line_breaks+task_lists
summary: |
    <video controls="controls" autoplay="autoplay" loop="loop" preload="metadata">
      <source src="/2026/05/19/kakehashi-vscode/video/demo.en.mp4" type="video/mp4">
    </video>
---

[kakehashi]: https://github.com/atusy/kakehashi

[kakehashi.nvim]: https://github.com/atusy/kakehashi.nvim

[kakehashi] lets you use intelligent features like completion and hover on source code embedded in another language — for example, the code blocks inside Markdown.

I built a [VSCode extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=atusy.kakehashi-vscode) so you can use [kakehashi] from VSCode too!
Here's how it works in action. Give it a try!

<video controls="controls" autoplay="autoplay" loop="loop" preload="metadata">
  <source src="video/demo.en.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

By the way, Markdown is just an example — kakehashi can be extended to a variety of document formats.
I'll save that story for another post...

## How to install

1. Install kakehashi (CLI)
    - Install via cargo or brew
        - `brew install atusy/tap/kakehashi`
        - `cargo install --git https://github.com/atusy/kakehashi`
    - Adjust your `PATH` environment variable if necessary
1. Configure kakehashi (CLI)
    - Create `~/.config/kakehashi/kakehashi.toml`
    - Configure the language servers for the languages you want to use with kakehashi

        ```toml
        # kakehashi.toml
        [languageServers.basedpyright]
        cmd = ["basedpyright-langserver", "--stdio"]
        languages = ["python"]

        [languageServers.r_language_server]
        cmd = [ "R", "--no-echo", "-e", "languageserver::run()"]
        languages = ["R", "r"]
        ```

1. Install kakehashi (extension)
    - Search for `kakehashi` in the Marketplace, or run `code --install-extension atusy.kakehashi-vscode`
1. Configure VSCode (`settings.json`)
    - Open the command palette with `Ctrl+Shift+P` and run `Preferences: Open User Settings (JSON)`
    - Add `["markdown"]` and `"kakehashi.documentSelector"` as shown below


        ```json
        {
          "[markdown]": { "editor.quickSuggestions": { "other": "on" } },
          "kakehashi.documentSelector": [ "markdown" ]
        }
        ```

### If you want to use it with Quarto or R Markdown

- Add configuration to kakehashi (CLI)
    ```toml
    [languages.qmd]
    base = "markdown"
    [languages.rmd]
    base = "markdown"
    ```
- Add configuration to VSCode
    - Install the required extensions ([quarto.quarto](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=quarto.quarto) / [REditorSupport.r](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=REditorSupport.r))
    - Add `"rmd"` or `"quarto"` to `kakehashi.documentSelector` in `settings.json`

## ENJOY!!

If you'd like to learn more about kakehashi itself or the extension, please check the README in each repository.
Stars are a huge motivation booster!!

- <https://github.com/atusy/kakehashi>
- <https://github.com/atusy/kakehashi-vscode>

As an R user you might be wondering about Positron support — it's not supported yet!
Stay tuned!!

Sponsorship is also very welcome!

- <https://github.com/sponsors/atusy>
